description:
Symptom: ./target/release/fz2 build --log-telemetry on fixtures/quicksort/input.fz sometimes runs 332 jobs (2.5MB log), sometimes 54,203+ jobs (727MB, killed) — same binary, same input. The log is the messenger: the activation fixpoint enters a self-sustaining period-2 oscillation.

THE OSCILLATOR — activation A = qsort/1 [Ty(46)] (fn33), verified from quicksort.really-bad.log + quicksort.better.log event timelines:
- A's analysis reads its OWN previous return R via Current(ReturnType(A)) — self-recursive callsites cs1/cs2 go through prepare_function_call (jobs/semantic.rs:1826-1842), which does reads.push(ReturnType(key)) and activation_return(key).unwrap_or(none): ABSENT IS READ AS none (unknown-as-none).
- A's append callsite cs3 args depend on R: append(qsort(lo)=R, [p|qsort(hi)]). canonical_activation_key (world.rs:2008) keeps dispatch-relevant slots un-collapsed (append dispatches [] vs [h|t] on arg0), so the append ACTIVATION KEY FLIPS with R: R=Ty(37)=empty-list -> append_B=[Ty37,Ty42]; R=Ty(46)=list(int) -> append_A=[Ty46,Ty42].
- Each completion REPLACES the job's activation-input contributions (complete_job -> replace_activation_input_contributions -> ActivationInputMap::replace, keyed by publisher job). Contributing append_B drops append_A; if A is the SOLE publisher, Activation(append_A)+ActivationInputs(append_A) are RETRACTED (facts.rs:195-207, last publisher leaves -> slot dropped).
- The retraction wakes AnalyzeActivation(append_A), which finds activation_inputs()=None (world.rs:391 gates on fact revision) and returns wait_on_current with EMPTY outputs (jobs/semantic.rs:88-90) -> replace_outputs retracts ITS ReturnType too (destructive wait).
- Beat: run reading R=Ty(37) publishes Ty(46) (append_B present, ret Ty(39)) and retracts append_A; run reading R=Ty(46) finds ReturnType(append_A) absent -> none -> cs3 ret none -> publishes Ty(37) (define_activation_return is last-write-wins: ActivationMap::define_return, semantic.rs:107 — DESCENT allowed) and re-adds append_A. Every publication flips content -> revision bump -> self-wake via the Current(ReturnType(A)) subscription -> next run. 2 A-runs per cycle, forever. Counts match: A ran 21,570x, ReturnType(A) revised every run; append_A's Activation/ActivationInputs retracted+republished 10,782x each; append's analysis ran 10,782x publishing its return ONCE.

THE ESCAPE (why good runs settle): activation B = qsort[Ty(28)] also reads ReturnType(A) (its self-call arg widens to Ty(46)). If B samples the Ty(46) phase, B's append args become [Ty46,Ty39] -> B contributes append_A as a SECOND publisher -> A's flips can no longer retract it -> ReturnType(append_A)=Ty(39) survives -> next A-run reading R=Ty(46) computes Ty(46), changed=false, no self-wake, quiescent. better.log shows exactly this: B's final cs3 flips to [Ty46,Ty39]->Ty(39), then A publishes Ty(46) twice and stops.

THE TRAP (why bad runs never escape): FIFO agenda + enqueue coalescing PHASE-LOCK B — ReturnType(A) changes twice per cycle but B coalesces to one run per cycle, always sampling the Ty(37) phase (bad-run B's cs1 reads Ty(37) for 10k+ consecutive runs). Once locked, the loop is fully deterministic. The randomness is only at ENTRY: dependent wake order iterates HashSet<J> (deps.rs:88-100 subscribers/waiters) and follow_up HashSet<Job> (jobs/semantic.rs:315), RandomState-seeded per process. Some seeds settle, some lock — nothing in between, matching the bimodal logs.

FOUR COMPOUNDING DEFECTS (fix any one and the cycle breaks; the principled frame is the fz-rh2.18 current-vs-settled epic):
1. unknown-as-none at prepare_function_call (violates 'unknown is not none').
2. last-write-wins ReturnType — non-monotone; a join (union(Ty37,Ty46)=Ty46) converges in one step, at the cost of never narrowing on true retraction.
3. destructive waits — early-wait effects with empty outputs retract the job's still-valid published facts.
4. contribution sets derived from mid-fixpoint Current reads retract sibling activations' demand facts (sole-publisher flips).
Also explains compiler2 quicksort drive tests intermittently running >60s under cargo test. Likely same family as fz-8b0 planner widening note (none eff_ret).
title: compiler2 semantic fixpoint non-convergence: recursive activation return oscillates; schedule-order non-determinism decides settling
